data.title: Live Nation gains after court hearing on DOJ's antitrust lawsuit ends
data.description:
Live Nation (LYV) rose 5.8% after a court hearing over the company's attempt to have the Dept. of Justice's antitrust lawsuit thrown out ended. 

The court hearing in Manhattan, before Judge Arun Subramanian, ended on Friday without a ruling from the judge, a court observer told Seeking Alpha. The judge didn't indicate when he would make a ruling. 

The concert and ticketing company was trying to get a summary judgement that would end the government's case or decide the case without need for a jury trial.  

In 2024, the DOJ and more than two dozen states filed an antitrust suit against Ticketmaster parent Live Nation (LYV), looking to break up the company. The lawsuit alleged that an illegal monopoly on live events pushes prices higher for fans.

data.title: Cohen & Steers signals 8% REIT earnings growth for 2026 amid strategic distribution expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Cohen & Steers (CNS) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Joseph Harvey, CEO, highlighted that "we ended 2025 with good momentum across key business metrics. We saw positive flows into nearly all vehicles. Fee rates were stable. Our institutional pipeline continued to strengthen and we are making progress on distribution initiatives." Harvey noted net inflows of $1.28 billion for the quarter and $1.5 billion for the year, driven by advisory, U.S. REITs, global listed infrastructure, and a $513 million inflow from a rights offering for the infrastructure closed-end fund. He also emphasized initiatives around expanding global distribution, particularly the promotion of Dan Noonan to Head of Global Distribution, and a focus on increasing coverage in the RIA channel, sub-advisory, and international institutional presence.
  • Michael Donohue, Interim CFO, stated "reported earnings of $0.81 per share, which equaled EPS reported in the prior quarter. Earnings for the full year 2025 was $3.09 per share compared to $2.93 in 2024." Donohue added, "Revenue for Q4 increased 2% sequentially to $143.8 million. Revenues for the full year increased 6.9% versus the prior year to $554 million." He pointed out that net inflows made Q4 the fifth out of six trailing quarters with inflows and operating income rose 3% to $52.4 million during the quarter. Liquidity at year-end totaled $403 million.
  • John Cheigh, President & Chief Investment Officer, highlighted long-term outperformance, stating "on a 1-year basis, 95% of our AUM has outperformed its benchmark, while our 3-, 5- and 10-year outperformance rates are all above 95%." Cheigh also noted, "our nontraded REIT, CNS REIT, has enjoyed a 10.3% annualized return since its January 2024 inception, which is more than double the return of the median equity nontraded REIT."

Outlook

  • Donohue indicated, "we would expect our compensation ratio to remain at 40%. We continue to maintain a disciplined approach to managing talent by balancing our business needs and strategic priorities with revenue growth." He further noted, "we expect annual G&A growth in 2026 to moderate from 2025 and are projecting it to be in the mid-single-digit percentage range."
  • Regarding tax guidance, Donohue said, "we expect our effective tax rate to be 25.4% on an as-adjusted basis."
  • Cheigh forecasted, "we expect the combination of lower supply and accelerating economic growth and thus demand to result in accelerating REIT earnings above trend to roughly 8% in 2026 and 2027."

Financial Results

  • Donohue reported, "Revenue for Q4 increased 2% sequentially to $143.8 million," and "operating income increased 3% to $52.4 million during the quarter." He noted, "ending AUM in Q4 was $90.5 billion, which was down slightly from the end of Q3. However, as noted earlier, we experienced higher average AUM during Q4 as compared to the prior quarter."
  • Net inflows during Q4 were $1.2 billion, primarily related to advisory and closed-end funds, offset by market depreciation and distributions. The compensation ratio decreased to 39% for the quarter.
  • Liquidity at year-end totaled $403 million, representing a $39 million increase versus the prior quarter end.

Q&A

  • John Dunn, Evercore ISI: "I wanted to ask a little more on the private real estate. It seems like we're seeing signs of some improving demand for that asset class. First of all, are you seeing that? And then also, do you think it can be a more significant contributor in 2026?" CEO Harvey responded, "I think we're early in the process of investor interest coming back to private real estate, but we're seeing some good early phase signs... We're gaining critical mass and we're starting to broaden the number of platforms that we're on. So we're ready in case that transition does follow through."
  • Dunn: "Do you think active ETFs can scale more quickly for you guys?" Harvey answered, "Absolutely... there are core strategies. They're just delivered in a different vehicle and the market has voted that active ETFs are the way to go for the future... we've got our milestones. We want to get the vehicles as we launch them to scale so that allocators know they can come in and have efficient trading spreads on them."
  • Rodrigo Ferreira, BofA Securities: "Can you talk a little bit about the recent progress in the institutional channel?" Harvey said, "we've now had a very strong pipeline for 2 quarters in a row. This quarter, I'd say that, that pipeline has broadened a little bit. It's deepened a little bit with the number of mandates, the location of the domicile of the allocator and the range of strategies."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a neutral-to-positive tone, with questions focusing on scaling opportunities for private real estate and ETFs, and institutional channel progress, seeking clarity on potential growth areas.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, reiterating strong performance and future potential. Cheigh remarked, "we continue to meet our objective of providing long-term alpha for our clients." Harvey was optimistic about distribution initiatives and product momentum.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management sustained a similar constructive tone, with management slightly more bullish regarding product scalability and institutional pipeline depth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 guidance language maintained stability, with compensation and tax ratios aligned to previous projections but with a more explicit projection for G&A growth moderation.
  • Strategic focus shifted toward realizing returns on recent investments, particularly in active ETFs, private real estate, and expansion in global distribution.
  • Analysts in both quarters centered questions on product performance, distribution, and institutional flows, but Q4 saw more discussion about rapid scaling of ETFs and early signs of private real estate demand.
  • Management's confidence increased around pipeline depth, scalability, and readiness for new product adoption, compared to a more cautious outlook in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted increased G&A expenses due to travel, business development, and talent acquisition costs.
  • Harvey acknowledged "known terminations" in client accounts but stated these have transitioned to a more typical low level of activity.
  • Private credit market shifts and competition remain a factor affecting flows, particularly in preferred strategies.

Final Takeaway

Cohen & Steers concluded 2025 with strong momentum across key products and channels, supported by net inflows, stable fee rates, and a deepening institutional pipeline. Management expressed optimism for continued growth in 2026, particularly in REIT earnings, private real estate, and scalable ETF strategies, with targeted investments in global distribution and a disciplined approach to costs and talent. The company believes it is well positioned to capitalize on shifting asset allocations and market leadership, aiming for accelerated growth and enhanced returns on recent strategic initiatives.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Independent Bank outlines 2026 profitability targets with 1.4% ROA and 15% ROTCE amid stable credit and C&I loan growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Independent Bank Corp. (INDB) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Jeffrey Tengel, President and CEO, highlighted that "our fourth quarter results reflect ongoing progress towards restoring Rockland Trust's historically strong performance. Quarterly highlights included continued NIM expansion, strong C&I growth, solid non deposit growth, stable credit costs, realized cost savings from the Enterprise acquisition and the return of excess capital to shareholders." Tengel noted a 60% increase in operating EPS and a 40 basis point rise in operating ROA between Q1 and Q4 2025, as well as a completed integration of Enterprise with nearly full retention of client-facing staff and minimal customer attrition.
  • On the commercial side, Tengel stated, "C&I loans increased 9% organically in 2025 and now represent 25% of total loans versus 22% at year-end '24." He also emphasized strong deposit retention, with "all Enterprise branches have exceeded our 95% deposit retention target" and highlighted new business relationships and stable wealth management AUA at $9.2 billion.
  • Tengel described 2025 as "busy and rewarding," noting cost controls, the launch of an AI innovation team, and a focus on organic growth, expense management, and capital optimization for 2026.
  • Mark Ruggiero, Chief Financial Officer, stated, "2025 fourth quarter GAAP net income was $75.3 million and diluted earnings per share was $1.52, resulting in a 1.20% return on assets, an 8.8% return on average common equity and a 12.77% return on average tangible common equity." Excluding merger expenses, adjusted operating net income was $84.4 million or $1.70 diluted EPS. Ruggiero emphasized, "net interest income increased $9.1 million to $212.5 million for the quarter. The reported margin increased 15 basis points to 3.77%, while the adjusted margin...increased 10 basis points to 3.64%."

Outlook

  • Ruggiero provided 2026 targets: "The first is return on average assets of 1.4% and the second is return on average tangible capital of 15%."
  • C&I loan growth is targeted at mid-single-digit percentages, CRE and construction at low single digits, and total consumer loans at flat to low single digits. Core deposit growth is expected in the low- to mid-single-digit range.
  • Net interest margin is projected to grow to a range of 3.85% to 3.90% by Q4 2026, assuming two Federal Reserve rate cuts and current yield curve stability. Noninterest income growth is guided to low single digits, with operating expenses estimated at $550 million to $555 million, plus $4 million to $5 million for the core system upgrade.
  • The bank expects overall asset quality metrics to remain stable, with no significant loss exposures currently in workout status.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter GAAP net income was $75.3 million and diluted EPS was $1.52, while adjusted operating net income was $84.4 million and $1.70 diluted EPS.
  • Net interest income was $212.5 million for the quarter, with a 3.77% reported margin and a 3.64% adjusted margin.
  • Total period end deposit balances declined 0.8% for the quarter, attributed to seasonal business activity, but average deposits grew, with average core deposits up 3.6% annualized.
  • Tangible book value grew by $1.04 to $47.55 at year-end. The company repurchased approximately 548,000 shares for $37.5 million in the quarter.
  • Total nonperforming assets were $85.7 million or 0.45% of total loans. Net charge-offs were $5.3 million, with $4 million related to a single C&I relationship. Provision for loan loss was $4.75 million.
  • Total fourth quarter operating expenses were $142 million, with core expenses plus cost saves from Enterprise around $136 million for the quarter.

Q&A

  • Jared David Shaw, Barclays: Asked about office credit dynamics. Ruggiero explained, "the most notable mover in terms of NPAs versus prior quarter is one specific loan...that's one relationship that was actually originally scheduled to mature last quarter...the P&S that is in process suggests a small loss there. So we did reserve about a $2 million loss in the fourth quarter."
  • Shaw queried deposit betas. Ruggiero responded, "I think fourth quarter was a really good example of our ability to do that...the combined methodology gets you to that 20%, give or take, all-in deposit base -- beta on the non-time deposits."
  • Shaw asked about M&A. Tengel confirmed, "we're really not focused on M&A at the moment. The priorities are organic growth, launching our expenses and focused on the conversion that's coming towards the latter part of the year."
  • Mark Fitzgibbon, Piper Sandler: Inquired about capital targets. Ruggiero replied, "long-term capital targets for us, CET1, probably in the high 11% to 12% range...tangible capital in the 8.75% to 9% range."
  • Fitzgibbon asked about non-accruals and credit cycle. Tengel said, "I would say like 8 or I don't know, Mark and I haven't talked about this...we might be making the call into the bull pen soon."
  • Stephen Moss, Raymond James: Asked about loan pricing and securities deployment. Ruggiero noted, "in some C&I deals, you're seeing some of the spreads competitively bidding out under 200 basis points. But we're getting our fair share of deal flow at the pricing we would like, which is 200 plus."
  • Moss also discussed hiring plans. Tengel indicated, "A number of the people that we hired in the second half of last year came over into a relatively new segment of the commercial business...we feel pretty good about their ability to drive activity and drive volume."
  • Laura Havener Hunsicker, Seaport Research Partners: Asked about expenses and system upgrade charges. Ruggiero confirmed, "the $4 million to $5 million of onetime, that's going to be spread over the year or sort of over the first 2, 3 quarters."
  • Hunsicker inquired about office non-performers. Ruggiero clarified, "That's one loan...we actually have a P&S on, where we accepted a slightly lower value than what the appraisal suggested. So that $2 million loss that we expect is already reserved for."
  • David Konrad, KBW: Asked about commercial banking platform growth and the exit from the floor plan business. Tengel explained, "we're in the middle...of exiting that business. It's around $100 million, give or take."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was generally neutral, with questions focusing on credit quality, deposit betas, capital deployment, and expense management. Analysts pressed for specifics on office exposures and nonperforming loans.
  • Management maintained a confident, steady tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing stability and forward momentum. Phrases such as "we feel good about the office book" and "we expect that to get resolved here early in 2026" support this view.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was similarly constructive but more forward-looking in outlining specific 2026 profitability targets. Analyst tone remained probing but measured.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 is more detailed, with explicit ROA and ROTCE targets and margin expansion projections, compared to the previous quarter's focus on integration and initial cost saves.
  • Strategic focus has shifted from integration and stabilization post-Enterprise acquisition to organic growth, expense management, and technology investment, including AI.
  • Analysts continued to focus on credit quality, office exposures, capital use, and deposit trends, but the Q4 call included more discussion of forward-looking profitability and business optimization.
  • Key metric changes include a higher operating EPS and margin, improved asset quality stability, and increased capital return to shareholders.
  • Management's confidence appears strengthened, with clearer articulation of profitability and growth targets, and a stated willingness to deploy excess capital prudently.

Risks and Concerns

  • Office loan exposures remain a point of scrutiny, with management describing challenges as "identifiable and manageable" and providing for anticipated losses.
  • Seasonal deposit declines, competitive loan pricing, and low growth in certain segments are noted.
  • Execution risk around the upcoming core system conversion and ongoing expense control is acknowledged, with a "hold-the-line" approach to staffing and targeted technology spending.
  • Analyst concerns focus on credit cycle timing, nonperforming assets, and the pace of organic growth relative to capital levels.

Final Takeaway

Management signaled a transition from integration to optimization, with detailed profitability targets for 2026, a stable credit outlook, and continued discipline in capital and expense management. The company expects to leverage its expanded footprint, commercial lending momentum, and technology investments to drive sustainable growth, while remaining attentive to credit risks and market competition.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: BlackRock’s Rick Rieder odds for Fed chair post surges - report
data.description:
BlackRock (BLK) executive Rick Rieder is emerging as a leading contender in the race for the next Federal Reserve chair, the Financial Times reported on Friday.

Rieder is BlackRock's chief investment officer of global fixed income, head of the fundamental fixed income business, and head of the global allocation investment team.

The odds of Rieder getting picked by Donald Trump surged from 6% earlier this week to 34% as of Friday morning amid speculations that the U.S. president will back a candidate with close links to Wall Street to replace Fed Chair Jerome Powell, the January 23 report by the FT noted.

Kalshi prediction market traders had nearly doubled the odds for Rieder getting Trump's nomination to 33% from where they were at the beginning of the week, CNBC News had reported on Thursday.

Earlier this week, Trump had reportedly said he will announce his nominee to lead the central bank "in the not too distant future".

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: DoorDash, Uber rejected in bid to block NYC tipping law
data.description:
DoorDash (DASH) and Uber Technologies (UBER) were unsuccessful in their attempt to block the implementation of a New York City law that requires a tipping option be given to customers at checkout.

"Plaintiffs have not made the required showing of a likelihood of success on the merits, irreparable harm, or that the balance of equities and public interest favor the extraordinary remedy of preliminary relief," said New York US District Judge George Daniels in his ruling.

Beginning next week, a new NYC law will require delivery apps to prompt customers to include a tip of at least 10% to ensure minimum earnings for delivery drivers after a new minimum wage law went into effect in 2023.

Delivery companies like Uber (UBER) and DoorDash (DASH) argue that a minimum gratuity forces customers to give a tip when one is neither earned nor necessary, while others claim that if a customer doesn’t leave a large enough tip at the beginning of the transaction, drivers won’t take the order.

“Forcing platforms to solicit a tip before checkout at a time when New Yorkers are sick of tipping culture and facing a growing affordability crisis is bad policy—plain and simple,” a DoorDash representative said to the New York Post.

Judge Daniels decision follows an allegation from the New York City Department of Consumer and Worker Protection ((DWCP)) that Uber Eats (UBER) and DoorDash (DASH) designed their platform to discourage tipping.

“Forcing people to tip may as well be a tax. It should be up to consumers, not politicians, whether they want to tip more in New York after already paying for a billion-dollar raise for workers," DoorDash’s head of North American policy, John Horton, said in a statement to Seeking Alpha.

According to a survey by WalletHub, nearly 90% of respondents believe tipping culture has “gotten out of control,” while 30% tip less when prompted for a suggested tip.

data.title: Smart eyewear firm Solos sues Meta, EssilorLuxottica for patent infringement: Bloomberg
data.description:
Meta Platforms Inc. (META) and its smart glasses partner EssilorLuxottica SA are being sued by Solos Technology Ltd., a smaller tech eyewear company that is alleging patent infringement, Bloomberg reported on Friday.

The report said Solos filed the complaint in a federal Massachusetts court and is seeking "multiple billions of dollars" in damages and an injunction.

The infringement filing mostly focuses on the Ray-Ban Meta Wayfarer Gen 1 but says, “Defendants’ later releases are derivative of the Gen 1 platform and continue to implement Solos’ patented technologies, resulting in ongoing and continuous infringement,” the report said.

Solos also claims that Oakley employees were introduced to its preliminary glasses technology as early as 2015, with a former senior executive later receiving a commercial pair for testing purposes in 2019.

Similarly, the complaint alleges that EssilorLuxottica met with Solos personnel throughout 2017 and gained knowledge of the company’s concepts and roadmap.

As for Meta, Solos alleges that MIT Sloan Fellow Priyanka Shekar published a 2021 research study titled “Audio Wearable Product Strategy: Expanding User Experience for Solos Smart Glasses” and then brought that knowledge to Meta when she joined the company as a product manager. Her study cited Solos’ patents as one of the company’s strengths.

“By the time Meta jointly commercialized smart-glasses products in or around 2021 with EssilorLuxottica, both sides had accumulated years of direct, senior-level, and increasingly detailed knowledge of Solos’ smart-glasses technology,” the complaint said.

data.title: Novo Nordisk Wegovy pill uptake negatively impacts syringe makers
data.description:
• Becton, Dickinson (BDX) and West Pharmaceutical Services (WST), two of the largest manufacturers of syringes, are down Friday following strong initial prescription figures for Novo Nordisk's (NVO) oral Wegovy (semaglutide) pill for weight loss. 
  • The decline is due to the perception that oral GLP-1s could cannibalize GLP-1s that are administered via injection.
  • BD is off ~2%, while West Pharmaceutical is down ~5%.
  • JP Morgan's Robbie Marcus, who has a neutral rating on BD, noted that its syringe exposure for GLP-1s is less than 2% of sales. "While strength in oral GLP-1s could reduce the size of the total opportunity versus a world with injectables alone, we still expect injectables will have an important role in the treatment paradigm with orals mostly used for maintenance."

data.title: Tech Voices: Nvidia China chip sales, Amazon layoffs, TikTok
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact the technology sector.

  • Chinese officials have informed some of China's leading tech companies that they can prepare to place orders for Nvidia's  (NVDA) H200 AI chips, which the U.S. government recently approved for shipment to that country.

According to Bloomberg, Beijing is allowing Alibaba (BABA), Tencent (TCEHY) (TCTZF), and ByteDance (DNCE) to begin discussing order details, but will also encourage them to buy domestically produced chips as a condition for final approval.

  • Amazon (AMZN) plans to lay off around 14,000 workers beginning next week as part of a larger plan to trim its workforce by approximately 30,000 people.

The tech giant eliminated roughly 14,000 jobs last year during its first round of layoffs. The second round, which could begin as early as Tuesday, is expected to impact a similar number of jobs, according to Reuters, which cited people close to the matter.

Divisions and units slated for cuts include retail, People Experience and Technology, Amazon Web Services, and Prime Video, Reuters added.

  • President Trump praised the establishment of a new, American-run joint venture to oversee TikTok's U.S. operations.

"I am so happy to have helped in saving TikTok! It will now be owned by a group of Great American Patriots and Investors, the Biggest in the World, and will be an important Voice," said Trump in a post late Thursday on Truth Social.

"Along with other factors, it was responsible for my doing so well with the Youth Vote in the 2024 Presidential Election. I only hope that long into the future I will be remembered by those who use and love TikTok," Trump continued.

"I would also like to thank President Xi, of China, for working with us and, ultimately, approving the Deal. He could have gone the other way, but didn’t, and is appreciated for his decision," Trump added.

data.title: Eastern Bankshares signals 3–5% loan growth and focuses on organic expansion while targeting 12% CET1 ratio
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Eastern Bankshares, Inc. (EBC) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Robert Rivers, Executive Chairman of the Board, highlighted that "2025 was a terrific year for Eastern, highlighted by a 62% increase in operating earnings, strong organic loan growth and a record level of wealth assets under management." He emphasized the merger with HarborOne as a milestone, strengthening the presence in key markets and providing entry into Rhode Island. Rivers stated, "As a result, we will not pursue any acquisitions as we are completely focused on organic growth and returning capital to our shareholders for the foreseeable future."
  • CEO Denis Sheahan echoed the focus on organic growth, stating "We have plenty of opportunities to organically grow the company's earnings and enhance profitability, and that is our focus." Sheahan noted the company's strategy to allocate capital towards organic growth and returning excess capital through share repurchases and dividends.
  • Sheahan underscored successful integration of new talent and the commercial lending platform, noting "We originated $2.5 billion of commercial loans in 2025... pipelines remain solid heading into 2026."
  • Sheahan also reported that wealth assets reached a record high of $10.1 billion at year-end, with $9.6 billion in assets under management.
  • CFO R. Rosato stated, "Q4 marked a strong finish to the year as we reported net income of $99.5 million or $0.46 per diluted share... Operating earnings of $94.7 million increased 28% linked quarter. On a per diluted share basis, operating earnings increased 19% to $0.44."

Outlook

  • The company provided a full year 2026 outlook, projecting loan growth of 3% to 5% and deposit growth of 1% to 2%. Net interest income is anticipated to be in the range of $1.20 billion to $1.50 billion with a full year FTE margin of 3.65% to 3.75%.
  • Provision expense for 2026 is expected to be $30 million to $40 million. Operating noninterest income is expected between $190 million and $200 million, and operating noninterest expense should be in the range of $655 million to $675 million.
  • The company intends to manage its CET1 ratio towards 12% and continue significant capital return through share repurchases and dividends. Management reiterated a strategic stance of not pursuing acquisitions and focusing on organic growth.

Financial Results

  • Net income for Q4 was $99.5 million, or $0.46 per diluted share. Operating earnings reached $94.7 million.
  • Operating ROA for the fourth quarter was 130 basis points, while return on average tangible common equity was 13.8%.
  • Net interest income was $237.4 million. The margin was 3.61% for the quarter, up from 3.47% in Q3.
  • Noninterest income for Q4 was $46.1 million, with mortgage banking income increasing to $3 million. Investment advisory fees rose to $18.6 million, and interest rate swap income reached $1.4 million.
  • Noninterest expense totaled $189.4 million, with nonoperating expenses of $33.4 million primarily due to merger-related costs and a $3.5 million lease impairment.
  • Deposits at period-end totaled $25.5 billion, an increase reflecting the HarborOne addition. Loans increased $4.7 billion linked quarter, primarily from the merger.
  • Allowance for loan losses was $332 million, or 144 basis points of total loans, up from $233 million at the end of Q3.

Q&A

  • Feddie Strickland, Hovde Group, LLC, Research Division, asked about margin trajectory. Rosato responded that "Our margin forecast does ramp up each -- marginally each quarter, accelerates a little bit in the back half of the year."
  • Strickland also inquired about pipeline mix for loan growth. Sheahan explained the pipeline is "about a little bit more than 50% CRE, between CRE and community development lending and the other, say, 45% is C&I."
  • Damon Del Monte, KBW, asked about provision outlook. Rosato stated, "The guidance is similar to what we gave last year. And then in '25, we outperformed the guidance. Our thoughts are generally the same."
  • Gregory Zingone, Piper Sandler, sought a breakdown of AUM growth. Sheahan replied, "We had about $200 million of net flows in the fourth quarter, really strong, good momentum building." Zingone also asked about nonperforming credits, and Rosato clarified, "They are not located in downtown Boston. The NPLs were completely driven by HarborOne... There's no surprises in there whatsoever to us."
  • Laura Havener Hunsicker, Seaport Research, questioned the lease impairment and nonperforming loans. Rosato confirmed it was a one-time event and discussed the timeline for resolving nonperforming loans, stating, "I would expect similar experience here, maybe even a little faster" compared to previous integrations.
  • Sun Young Lee, TD Cowen, asked about fee income and capital management. Rosato said fee income from HarborOne's mortgage business will "probably be 8% to 10% of total fee income" and discussed ongoing plans to manage the CET1 ratio towards 12%.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for details on margin trajectory, provision guidance, nonperforming loans, and capital deployment, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone, with a focus on clarity and future growth prospects.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone throughout, emphasizing organic growth, strong capital, and clear strategic priorities. Rosato and Sheahan repeatedly stressed their focus on organic expansion and returning capital.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts maintained a similar level of inquiry, while management's tone shifted from cautious optimism regarding M&A to a firmer stance against acquisitions and a stronger commitment to buybacks and organic growth, as highlighted by Sheahan: "We're not pursuing acquisitions. We're entirely focused on the growth of this company, the organic growth."

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The most significant change from Q3 is the definitive move away from M&A in favor of organic growth and share repurchases. The language regarding acquisitions became more definitive, with Sheahan stating they "will not pursue any acquisitions."
  • The capital return program accelerated, with $55.4 million in share repurchases in Q4, compared to a new repurchase authorization and only preparation for HarborOne integration in Q3.
  • The company realized notable increases in operating earnings, margins, and wealth assets, as well as a substantial rise in allowance for loan losses due to the HarborOne merger.
  • Guidance language became more specific regarding targets for loan and deposit growth, margin, and capital ratios.
  • Analyst focus remained on credit quality, margin trajectory, and capital management, but questions about M&A receded as management's stance became unequivocal.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified an increase in nonperforming loans and criticized/classified loans, primarily due to acquired HarborOne credits. Rosato noted, "Nonperforming loans increased as expected by $103 million linked quarter, mostly due to $94 million of loans acquired from HarborOne... Reserve levels remain strong as demonstrated by an allowance for loan losses of $332 million."
  • The company acknowledged slightly elevated deposit costs due to the HarborOne deposit base, but expects to manage these costs down over time.
  • Provision expense guidance for 2026 reflects a conservative stance but not underlying credit concerns.
  • The integration of HarborOne's mortgage operations and resolution of acquired nonperforming loans are ongoing focus areas.

Final Takeaway

Eastern Bankshares, Inc. closes 2025 with strong operating performance, highlighted by successful organic loan growth, record wealth management assets, and the completed HarborOne merger. Management has signaled a clear strategic pivot to prioritize organic growth and aggressive capital return, moving away from M&A activity. The company enters 2026 with a strong capital base, firm guidance on profitability and growth targets, and a commitment to returning meaningful value to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends while maintaining a clear focus on credit quality and operational integration.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Meta temporarily freezes teen access to AI characters to add more parental controls
data.description:
Meta Platforms Inc. (META) said Friday it is temporarily restricting teens around the world from accessing AI characters across its family of apps.

The company said that after hearing feedback from parents about wanting more data on their teen's interaction data with AI characters, it started building a new version to fulfill those requests.

Once ready, the new version of Meta's AI characters will include additional parental controls. 

Teens will still be able to access helpful information and educational opportunities through Meta’s AI assistant, with default, age-appropriate protections in place, the company said.

"Starting in the coming weeks, teens will no longer be able to access AI characters across our apps until the updated experience is ready. This will apply to anyone who has given us a teen birthday, as well as people who claim to be adults but who we suspect are teens based on our age prediction technology," the company said in an updated blog post.
